issues.claim: No claim that the Gita is really a tech manual
issues.objection: 7.30 explicitly preserves the verse as a verse: literal_meaning, traditional_meaning_consensus, verbatim Shankara/Ramanuja under fair-use, source pack with full sectarian attribution. Engineering layer is presented as operational reflection at narrower scope, with STRETCHED tagging where appropriate. Translation field opens by acknowledging the metaphysical reach (where applicable) before describing the operational analog. The verse is not flattened into a productivity nugget. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Chapter-closing verse. Heavy metaphysics: adhibhūta/adhidaiva/adhiyajña + prayāṇa-kāla. Forward-pointer to Ch 8 honored explicitly. Engineering analog scoped at career-end-departure substrate-articulation. Counter-example: performative all-hands talk that does not survive the team's actual problems is the failure mode.

notes: Tech-manual framing check for 7.30: the engineering layer is additive, not substitutive. PASS.
